title: Jamaican pepperpot soup
subtitle: Hearty meat and ground provisions soup
excerpt: A thick, spinach-dark soup of beef, salt pork, and ground provisions (cho cho, sweet potato, and yam), seasoned with scotch bonnet, allspice, and thyme, with okra stirred in at the end to thicken the broth. Jamaican pepperpot soup is not the spicy Guyanese dish of the same name; it is a restorative, deeply flavoured meat and vegetable pot built over a long simmer.
sourceNotes: Jamaican pepperpot soup has different origins from the Guyanese pepperpot stew, despite sharing the name. The Jamaican version is a meat and vegetable soup with callaloo or spinach as the green element and ground provisions (root vegetables) as the body. It appears in Jamaican cookbooks from the 20th century as a restorative Sunday soup, often made from the meat left over from the week.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the okra. Add fresh okra when reheating.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the day after making. The broth deepens overnight.

content.text: The soup is thickened with okra, which releases a natural mucilage when cooked that binds the broth to a silky consistency. Add the okra in the last 15 minutes — longer than this and it becomes slimy rather than silky. The ground provisions (cho cho, sweet potato) should be just tender but not collapsing.

Spinach stands in for callaloo leaves in this recipe. If you can find fresh callaloo or dasheen bush from a Caribbean food shop, use it instead — the flavour is more mineral and earthy than spinach and far more authentic.

Place the {{beef-chuck}} in a large pot with 1.5 litres of cold water. Bring to a boil, skim off the grey foam that rises, and reduce to a steady simmer. Add the {{spring-onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{thyme}}, {{allspice}}, {{scotch-bonnet}} (whole), {{black-pepper}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes. Add the {{spinach}}, {{cho-cho}}, and {{sweet-potato}}. Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes until the vegetables are tender. Add the {{okra}} and cook for a further 15 minutes. The broth should be thick and dark green. Remove the scotch bonnet before serving.

items.cause: Okra not added or not enough spinach
items.fix: Add more sliced okra and cook for 15 more minutes. The okra releases its thickening agent quickly

items.cause: Scotch bonnet broke or was left in too long
items.fix: Add the scotch bonnet whole and remove at the 1-hour mark. A broken scotch bonnet releases significantly more heat and bitterness

items.cause: Cooked too long or added too early
items.fix: Add the cho cho and sweet potato only after the meat has had 45 minutes. They need 30 minutes, not the full cook time

content.text: Pepperpot soup sits alongside the pumpkin soup and chicken soup as one of Jamaica's three great soup traditions. All three are Saturday soups in Jamaica — thick, rich, restorative pots that cook slowly through the morning and are served at midday. The name connects to a broader Caribbean tradition of pepperpot cooking that spans from Jamaica to Guyana, each version distinct despite the shared name. The Jamaican version is the most vegetable-forward of the regional pepperpots.
